tex:
Helga buys some items to do some knitting.

(a) Complete Helga's bill from one shop.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Item & Cost (\$) \\
\hline
2 pairs of knitting needles at \$4.95 a pair & \\
\hline
6 buttons at \$0.65 each & \\
\hline
1 knitting pattern at \$3.60 & 3.60 \\
\hline
 Total & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) Helga also buys 8 balls of wool from another shop.

Each ball costs \$3.12\,.

Helga pays with a \$50 note.

Work out the amount of change she receives.

(c) Helga knits some squares.

Each square is either white, pink or blue.

The number of squares are in the ratio white : pink : blue $= 5 : 3 : 2$.

30 squares are blue.

Show that Helga knits 150 squares.

(d) Helga uses some of the squares to make a rectangular blanket.

The blanket is 6 squares long and 4 squares wide.

(i)  Calculate the percentage of the 150 squares she uses to make this blanket.

 Each square has side length 15\,cm.

Work out the perimeter of this blanket.

Give your answer in metres.
texHtml:
Helga buys some items to do some knitting. (a) Complete Helga's bill from one shop.
ItemCost (\$)
2 pairs of knitting needles at \$4.95 a pair
6 buttons at \$0.65 each
1 knitting pattern at \$3.603.60
Total
(b) Helga also buys 8 balls of wool from another shop. Each ball costs \$3.12\,. Helga pays with a \$50 note. Work out the amount of change she receives. (c) Helga knits some squares. Each square is either white, pink or blue. The number of squares are in the ratio white : pink : blue $= 5 : 3 : 2$. 30 squares are blue. Show that Helga knits 150 squares. (d) Helga uses some of the squares to make a rectangular blanket. The blanket is 6 squares long and 4 squares wide. (i) Calculate the percentage of the 150 squares she uses to make this blanket. Each square has side length 15\,cm. Work out the perimeter of this blanket. Give your answer in metres.

Miguel works in an office.

(a) 
t It takes Miguel 40 minutes to drive to work.

(i)  He leaves home at 07\,45.

What time does he arrive at work?

 Miguel drives to work at an average speed of 57\,km/h.

Show that he drives 38\,km.

t White paper costs $w$ cents per sheet and pink paper costs $p$ cents per sheet.

Miguel uses 56 sheets of white paper and 21 sheets of pink paper.

Write down an expression, in terms of $w$ and $p$, for the total cost, in cents, of the paper he uses.

t Miguel has a closed box of pens.

The box is in the shape of a cuboid measuring 20\,cm by 12\,cm by 7\,cm.

Calculate the surface area of the box.

Miguel records the length of time of each telephone call he receives, correct to the nearest minute.

\begin{tabular}{cccccccccc}
7 & 15 & 6 & 28 & 8 & 21 & 17 & 19 & 20 & 12 \\
11 & 19 & 12 & 3 & 20 & 23 & 14 & 9 & 4 & 18 \\
\end{tabular}
(a) 
t Complete the frequency table.

You may use the tally column to help you.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time (minutes) & Tally & Frequency \\
\hline
$0 - 5$ & & \\
\hline
$6 - 10$ & & \\
\hline
$11 - 15$ & & \\
\hline
$16 - 20$ & & \\
\hline
$21 - 25$ & & \\
\hline
$26 - 30$ & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

t Draw a bar chart to show this information.

Complete the scale on the frequency axis.

t Use the bar chart to write down the modal group.

t Find

(i)  a multiple of 3 between 70 and 80,

 a factor of 63 between 5 and 10,

 a cube number between 60 and 90,

 the reciprocal of 7.

t Work out $\displaystyle\frac{2}{7}$ of 84.

t Find the value of

(i)  $\sqrt[3]{3375}$,

 $12^0$.

t Rana hires a car.

The cost is \$74 per day plus a delivery cost of \$17.50\,.

Rana pays a total of \$461.50\,.

Calculate the number of days that Rana hires the car.

t A train to town $A$ leaves a station every 25 minutes.

A train to town $B$ leaves the same station every 45 minutes.

Both trains leave at 08\,00.

Find the next time both trains leave together.

(a) 
t The table shows the number of items sold to each of 60 customers in a shop.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Number of items sold & Frequency \\
\hline
0 & 3 \\
\hline
1 & 6 \\
\hline
2 & 12 \\
\hline
3 & 8 \\
\hline
4 & 14 \\
\hline
5 & 10 \\
\hline
6 & 3 \\
\hline
7 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Find the range.

 Calculate the mean.

 Find the probability that a customer picked at random buys more than 4 items.

t Carlotta buys a bicycle.

(i)  The length, $l$\,cm, of the bicycle is 96\,cm, correct to the nearest centimetre.

Complete this statement about the value of $l$.

{---} $\leqslant l <$ ][]

 The diameter of each bicycle wheel is 46\,cm.

Carlotta rides the bicycle a distance of 1.4\,km.

Calculate the number of complete revolutions that a wheel makes during this journey.
